summary: Changed Player_GetDamage's return type from s16 to s32 in both src/melee/pl/player.c and src/melee/pl/player.h. The PR body states this was needed so other functions calling Player_GetDamage could match, while the function itself still matched and did not break existing code.
what_changed: Updated the implementation signature of Player_GetDamage in src/melee/pl/player.c from s16 Player_GetDamage(s32 slot) to s32 Player_GetDamage(s32 slot).
Updated the declaration of Player_GetDamage in src/melee/pl/player.h from s16 Player_GetDamage(s32 slot) to s32 Player_GetDamage(s32 slot).
No function body changes were shown in the diff; the change was limited to the return type in the source and header.
smart_moves: Kept the source definition and header prototype synchronized, avoiding mismatched declarations for callers.
Used a minimal ABI/signature adjustment to unblock matching in other call sites without perturbing the existing function body.
Preserved the existing matched implementation while correcting the type information exposed to dependent functions.
decomp_lessons: A function can still locally match with a too-narrow return type, but callers may fail to match because the compiler generates different extension, truncation, or register-use behavior based on the declared return type.
When caller functions have unexplained mismatch around return value handling, check the callee's prototype width in headers, not just the callee's own body.
For shared player APIs, keep src/melee/pl/player.c and src/melee/pl/player.h signatures aligned before attempting to tune callers.
Small type fixes can have project-wide matching impact even when they only change one declaration and one definition.
naming_conventions: Player module functions use the Player_ prefix, as in Player_GetDamage, Player_CheckSlot, Player_SwapTransformedStates, Player_GetEntity, and Player_SetHUDDamage.
Slot-indexed player helpers in this area take s32 slot parameters.
Project typedefs such as s16 and s32 are used for integer widths rather than raw C integer types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Adjust return type width from s16 to s32 when caller-side matching suggests the value is treated as a full 32-bit integer.
Prefer correcting the shared function prototype over adding local caller workarounds when multiple callers depend on the same return-value convention.
Use header prototype fixes as a matching tactic because caller code generation depends on the visible declaration.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body says the author believed the change should not need review because it did not break anything and still matched.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs or commits touching Player_GetDamage callers to identify which functions were unblocked by the s32 return type.
Search for other player.h prototypes where narrow return types may cause caller mismatches.
Check whether StaticPlayer damage storage is s16 or s32 to distinguish stored field width from API return width.
